title: Apply for a CARICOM–Colombia certificate of origin
description: Get a certificate of origin under the CARICOM–Colombia trade agreement.
summary: Barbados and CARICOM have a trade agreement with Colombia. The agreement gives qualifying Barbadian goods preferential customs treatment when they arrive in Colombia, instead of full duty.
The Certificate of Origin under this agreement is the proof that your goods qualify. It is issued by BIDC after you complete the form and provide supporting documents.
It is similar to the standard CARICOM certificate, but the rules of origin and the form layout are specific to the Colombia agreement. You will need to identify which rule of origin your goods meet.
If you regularly export to Colombia, talk to BIDC about getting set up with a streamlined process – it can save a lot of time on repeat shipments.

items: the consignee’s name and address in Colombia
the country of origin, country of destination and country of issue
transport information (vessel, aircraft, place of loading)
details of the goods, HS tariff number, value and gross weight

items.name: Bill of lading or airway bill
Proof that the goods meet the CARICOM–Colombia rules of origin

how_long: Certificates are usually issued within 3 to 5 working days.

options.heading: In person
content: Take the completed CARICOM–Colombia certificate of origin to BIDC at Pelican House.

contact.address:
Pelican House, Princess Alice Highway
St. Michael

assumptions: ‘Certificates are usually issued within 3 to 5 working days’ is an estimate.
‘Talk to BIDC about a streamlined process’ is general guidance, not a specific BIDC service.
